title: Garden Fresh Mediterranean Vegetable Tacos
description: A festive and flavorful vegetarian dish featuring roasted Mediterranean vegetables in hard taco shells, with a focus on spring ingredients and a hint of bitter flavors, all brought together with the precision of sous-vide cooking.
instructions: Preheat the sous-vide machine to 185 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the sliced eggplant and zucchini with salt and let them sit for 10 minutes to draw out excess moisture.
Rinse the eggplant and zucchini slices and pat them dry with paper towels.
Place the eggplant and zucchini slices in a single layer in a sous-vide bag, seal, and cook for 1 hour.
While the vegetables are cooking, roast the red bell pepper in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes, or until the skin is blistered and charred.
Peel the skin off the roasted red bell pepper and slice it into strips.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked eggplant, zucchini, roasted red bell pepper, Kalamata olives, artichoke hearts, and crumbled feta cheese.
Drizzle the lemon juice and olive oil over the vegetable mixture and toss to combine.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Warm the hard taco shells according to the package instructions.
Fill the warmed taco shells with the Mediterranean vegetable mixture and serve immediately.
